examples.title: Simple Shell Command Execution
examples.description: Demonstrates basic operation of a command interpreter (shell) that processes user commands
examples.code:
PROGRAM SimpleShell
BEGIN
  REPEAT
    DISPLAY "> "
    INPUT command
    IF command = "exit" THEN
      BREAK
    ELSE IF command = "ls" THEN
      CALL ListFiles()
    ELSE IF command = "date" THEN
      CALL ShowDate()
    ELSE IF command = "clear" THEN
      CALL ClearScreen()
    ELSE
      DISPLAY "Unknown command"
    END IF
  UNTIL FALSE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how a shell reads user commands, interprets them, and calls appropriate system functions. The shell loops continuously, accepting input until user types 'exit'.

examples.title: Process State Transition (Simplified)
examples.description: Demonstrates how OS manages process states during execution
examples.code:
PROGRAM ProcessScheduler
BEGIN
  PROCEDURE Schedule()
    FOR each process IN ReadyQueue DO
      IF process.state = "NEW" THEN
        process.state = "READY"
        ADD process TO ReadyQueue
      END IF
    END FOR

    WHILE ReadyQueue NOT empty DO
      currentProcess = REMOVE_FIRST(ReadyQueue)
      currentProcess.state = "RUNNING"
      ExecuteProcess(currentProcess)
      
      IF currentProcess.completed THEN
        currentProcess.state = "TERMINATED"
      ELSE IF currentProcess.waitingForIO THEN
        currentProcess.state = "WAITING"
        ADD process TO WaitingQueue
      ELSE
        currentProcess.state = "READY"
        ADD process TO ReadyQueue
      END IF
    END WHILE
  END PROCEDURE
END
examples.explanation: This shows basic process state transitions: NEW → READY → RUNNING → (WAITING or TERMINATED). The scheduler manages CPU allocation by moving processes between ready and running states.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of processes
